subject: Remove original exFAT driver.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3.8 Class G (GROUPED ROW: commits 8b6b8c2f5, df35bdb27, 858322ce6, 5220d6686, 7f7148c1f, 99a2c80d0), §N1

impact.effect_if_absent: The deletion of vanilla 5.15 exfat driver files has no effect if not carried, because vanilla 6.18 retains the exfat driver (same 14 files: fs/exfat/*.c|h with similar structure). Symlink support is provided separately via carried patch 0031 (exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch), which implements Samsung-compatible symlinks on vanilla exfat. FAT12/16/32 support is provided via vanilla vfat (CONFIG_VFAT_FS), not the Samsung driver.

notes: This commit was part of the fork's strategy to replace vanilla 5.15 exfat (14 files, no symlink support) with the out-of-tree Samsung/exfat-nofuse driver (20+ files, with symlinks for exFAT and FAT12/16/32 support). Patch-provenance.md §N1 and §3.8 document this replacement. The commit is NOT carried in our 6.18 build. Our approach (ADR 0010 + ADR 0019): (1) vanilla 6.18 exfat persists unchanged (same files as deleted here); (2) carried patch 0031 adds Samsung-compatible symlinks to vanilla exfat only; (3) vanilla vfat (CONFIG_VFAT_FS) handles FAT12/16/32 encoding via CONFIG_FAT_DEFAULT_UTF8=y. This trades the Samsung driver's multi-filesystem unified symlink support for maintainability via two vanilla upstream drivers. No exFAT functionality is lost (symlinks via patch 0031, encoding via mount options). FAT12/16/32 symlinks remain unsupported (design decision in ADR 0019). The fork's intent was to REMOVE vanilla exfat to enable Samsung driver integration; our 6.18 build deliberately KEEPS vanilla exfat and adds targeted symlink support instead via a separate carried patch — that is the reason for dropped-deliberate. upstream.equivalence corrected from 'contradicted' to null: this commit is a pure deletion (added=0, no vanilla_file_line/vanilla_quote grounding), so the equivalence enum (which grades behavioral equivalence of functionality a commit ADDS) does not apply — there is no added behavior for vanilla to be equivalent to or contradict, and 'contradicted' would incorrectly flag this dropped-deliberate deletion as a carry candidate per the pilot-lesson heuristic, which is wrong here. sonnet-verified: equivalence-flag semantics fixed per grounding contract (no upstream claim without a quote); disposition=dropped-deliberate and supersession by 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch confirmed correct.
